summary: Remove a scheduler-sensitive wall-clock assertion from the cold disk-cache unit while retaining its behavioral cache-before-engine assertions and aggregate performance evidence in the dedicated profile lane.

decisions.decision: The aggregate unit verifies cold-tier lookup behavior and engine suppression without a host-scheduler-sensitive 100 ms wall-clock assertion; latency claims remain in the dedicated viewer/performance evidence lane.
decisions.rationale: The assertion passed in isolation at 24 ms but failed twice only under 164-file parallel Vitest contention at 168–230 ms, creating a false red without any behavioral failure.

testEvidence.criterion: The cold disk-cache behavior remains green in isolation and in the aggregate suite after removing only the nondeterministic timing assertion.
testEvidence.test: Run the runtime unit file once and then the bounded aggregate suite once.

red.outcome: RED: 1 of 1,248 tests failed solely because the cold-cache wall-clock assertion measured 214.65 ms under parallel worker contention; cache hit and engine suppression assertions were not reached.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/runtime/workbench-runtime.test.ts; pnpm.cmd test -- --reporter=dot
green.outcome: GREEN: runtime file 27/27 passed; aggregate Test Files 164 passed (164), Tests 1248 passed (1248), Duration 18.96s.

testEvidence.criterion: The test-harness correction remains source-policy clean.
testEvidence.test: Run repository lint once after the bounded test-only correction.

red.outcome: RED: aggregate verification was not green before the timing assertion correction.

green.outcome: GREEN: Checked 429 files in 205ms with source policy passed.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
